title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
statement_text: For every integer $t \geq 1$, every graph $G$ with $\chi(G) = t$ contains a $K_t$-minor.
context_text: Hadwiger's conjecture is described as among the most famous and difficult open problems in graph theory. It has been verified for $t \leq 6$ (the cases $t = 5$ and $t = 6$ relying on the Four-Color Theorem), while all cases $t \geq 7$ remain wide open.
paper_contribution: Proves the $t = 5$ case as a corollary (Corollary 1.4) of establishing Holroyd's Strong Hadwiger's conjecture for $t = 4$; additionally shows that every 5-chromatic graph contains a $K_5$-minor with a singleton branch-set, and that in a 5-vertex-critical graph any specified vertex may serve as that singleton branch-set.
paper_title: Strengthening Hadwiger's conjecture for $4$- and $5$-chromatic graphs

title: Strong Hadwiger's Conjecture (Holroyd)
statement_text: Let $G$ be a graph with $\chi(G) = t$, and let $S$ be a colorful set in $G$. Then $G$ contains an $S$-rooted $K_t$-minor.
context_text: Holroyd proposed this strengthening of Hadwiger's conjecture in 1997 under the name 'Strong Hadwiger's conjecture', observing that a colorful set — one that meets every color class in every proper $\chi(G)$-coloring — should force a $K_t$-minor rooted at that set. Holroyd proved the conjecture for $t \leq 3$; the case $t = 4$ was the first open case.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the first open case $t = 4$ (Theorem 1.3): every graph $G$ with $\chi(G) = 4$ and colorful set $S \subseteq V(G)$ contains an $S$-rooted $K_4$-minor.
paper_title: Strengthening Hadwiger's conjecture for $4$- and $5$-chromatic graphs
